functions.name: dagster_cloud_cli.core.pex_builder.pex_registry.get_cached_deps_details
documentation:
Returns a metadata dict for the requirements_hash and cache_tag.

The dict contains:
'deps_pex_name': filename for the deps pex, eg 'deps-123234334.pex'
'dagster_version': dagster package version included in the deps, eg '1.0.14'

documentation: Saves the deps_pex_name and dagster_version into the requirements hash file.

documentation: python_version should includes the major and minor version only, eg Version('3.11').

functions.name: dagster_cloud_cli.core.pex_builder.util.build_pex
documentation:
Invoke pex with common build flags and pass parameters through to specific pex flags.

sources_directories: passed to the pex -D flag (aka --source-directories)
requirements_filepaths: passed to the pex -r flag (aka --requirement)

Platform:
For the pex --platform tag used below, see pex --help and
https://peps.python.org/pep-0425/
https://peps.python.org/pep-0427/

Packages for the current platform are only included if requested with PEX_INCLUDE_CURRENT_PLATFORM
The manylinux platform ensures pexes built on local machines (macos, windows) are compatible
with linux on cloud.

The python_version tuple eg ('3', '9') determines the target runtime environment. In theory
we can build a pex in an environment without the target python version present.

functions.name: dagster_cloud_cli.config_utils.write_config
documentation: Writes the given config object to the CLI config file.

functions.name: dagster_cloud_cli.config_utils.read_config
documentation: Reads the CLI config file into a config object.

functions.name: dagster_cloud_cli.config_utils.get_deployment
documentation:
Gets the configured deployment to target.
Highest precedence is a deployment argument, then `DAGSTER_CLOUD_DEPLOYMENT`
env var, then `~/.dagster_cloud_cli/config` default.

functions.name: dagster_cloud_cli.config_utils.get_organization
documentation:
Gets the configured organization to target.
Highest precedence is an organization argument, then `DAGSTER_CLOUD_ORGANIZATION`
env var, then `~/.dagster_cloud_cli/config` value.

functions.name: dagster_cloud_cli.config_utils.get_location_load_timeout
documentation:
Gets the configured location load timeout to target.
Highest precedence is an location-load-timeout argument, then `DAGTER_CLOUD_LOCATION_LOAD_TIMEOUT`
env var, then `~/.dagster_cloud_cli/config` value.

functions.name: dagster_cloud_cli.config_utils.get_agent_heartbeat_timeout
documentation:
Gets the configured agent timeout to target.
Highest precedence is an agent-timeout argument, then `DAGTER_CLOUD_AGENT_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT`
env var.

functions.name: dagster_cloud_cli.config_utils.get_user_token
documentation:
Gets the configured user token to use.
Highest precedence is an api-token argument, then `DAGSTER_CLOUD_API_TOKEN`
env var, then `~/.dagster_cloud_cli/config` value.

functions.name: dagster_cloud_cli.config_utils.available_deployment_names
documentation: Gets a list of deployment names given the Typer Context, used for CLI completion.

functions.name: dagster_cloud_cli.config_utils.get_url
documentation: Gets the url passed in or from the environment.

functions.name: dagster_cloud_cli.config_utils.dagster_cloud_options
documentation:
Apply this decorator to Typer commands to make them take the
`organization`, `deployment`, and `api-token` arguments.
These values are passed as keyword arguments.

Unless `allow_empty` or `allow_empty_deployment` are set, an error
will be raised if these values are not specified as arguments or via config/env var.

Set `requires_url` if the command needs a Dagster webserver url; if so, this will be provided
via the `url` keyword argument.
